name: June Oven
short_description: A Wi-Fi-connected countertop convection oven that uses an internal camera and machine learning to recognize food and run an automatic cook program.
long_description: The June Oven is a smart countertop appliance with an internal HD camera, a built-in scale, and a core-temperature probe, paired with computer-vision models that identify commonly cooked foods and select a cook program automatically. It is a clear example of physical AI hiding inside an everyday domestic object: rather than a robot or a humanoid, intelligence is embedded in a familiar appliance form, sensing and acting on the physical task of cooking. As the system cooks more food it improves its recognition, and it streams live and time-lapse video to a companion app. Originally launched by the startup June and later acquired by Weber, it shows how ambient AI can fold into the kitchen as a quiet, task-specific assistant.

images.caption: The June Oven, a countertop smart convection oven with an internal camera and touchscreen.
images.alt_text: A black stainless-steel countertop oven with a glass door and a small touchscreen on the front, photographed against a white background.
